label: The Quiet Gasket
description: The Quiet Gasket serves as the sleeping chamber set behind the shop’s faceted sales floor, sealed by a pressure-tight inner wall that blocks the rasp of glass dust. Its surfaces are clad in matte vitrified tile scored with grounding grooves, and a low berth is recessed into a shallow alcove to keep bedding below the drift line of charged particulates. A narrow slit-window of polarized mirror glass admits Lacrymara’s copper-white twilight in measured bands, doubling as a timekeeping gauge aligned to Seamglass Meridian’s calibrated causeways. Built-in cupboards of magnet-latched laminate store garments and field gear, their edges padded to prevent chime against the shop’s glasssteel stock.
